codeReview.summary: Clean implementation of copy-forward and change list in the scene graph library. SceneHeader extended correctly — the 52-byte _reserved field is replaced with change_count (u16, 2B) + changed_nodes ([NodeId; 24], 48B) + _reserved2 ([u8; 2], 2B) = 52B, preserving the 64-byte header size (verified by compile-time assert). All six new APIs (copy_front_to_back, mark_changed, change_list, is_full_repaint, clear sentinel, SceneWriter::new initialization) match the feature description exactly. Unsafe blocks have thorough SAFETY comments. Edge cases (overflow → FULL_REPAINT, mark after overflow is no-op, empty state, zero-length copies skipped) are handled correctly. The defensive .min(CHANGE_LIST_CAPACITY) guard in change_list() protects against corrupted change_count values. 16 new tests covering all 10 VAL-SCENE assertions.

issues.description: Dead variable with misleading name: `let data_used_before = dw.front_nodes().len();` reads node count (not data_used) and is never referenced in any assertion. The test actually uses `front_data_before` for validation. Should be removed to avoid confusing future readers.

mark_changed() allows duplicate NodeId entries (documented as intentional for O(1) perf). If a frame marks the same node multiple times (e.g., cursor position + cursor content change), this wastes change list capacity and could trigger premature FULL_REPAINT overflow. Consider adding a one-line comment near the call sites (in core, when wired) noting this contract: 'mark each node at most once per frame.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The copy-forward + change list pattern (copy_front_to_back → mutate specific nodes → mark_changed → swap) is a major new architectural pattern for the rendering pipeline. The existing architecture.md in .factory/library/ describes the current full-rebuild pipeline but doesn't document this incremental update path. Future workers implementing the compositor's change-list-aware rendering will need to understand this pattern.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: system/libraries/scene/lib.rs:668-733 (copy_front_to_back) establishes the pattern. AGENTS.md §Architecture Context references 'targeted update + change list' as the target pipeline but the library docs don't yet describe the concrete API.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md correctly documents the data buffer strategy (update_data for same-length, replace_data for changed-length, clear+reset_data at 75% threshold) and the well-known node indices. The change list capacity (24) and FULL_REPAINT sentinel semantics should be added to the Architecture Context section for compositor workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md §Data buffer strategy and §Architecture Context. CHANGE_LIST_CAPACITY=24 and FULL_REPAINT=u16::MAX are defined in scene/lib.rs but not referenced in AGENTS.md.

summary: Pass. The implementation is correct, well-tested (16 new tests, 88 total scene tests, full suite passing), and follows all project conventions. SceneHeader size is preserved at 64 bytes. All unsafe blocks have SAFETY comments. Two non-blocking observations: a dead test variable and a suggestion to document the 'mark each node once per frame' contract. Two shared state observations about documenting the copy-forward pattern and change list constants in library/architecture.md and AGENTS.md.
